title: VideoFactory
description: AssetFactory implementation that loads video files (MP4, WebM, OGG, and other browser-supported container formats) and produces a Video instance suitable for use as a dynamic texture source in the rendering pipeline. Video data is buffered via a `<video>` element rather than decoded up-front. The `'stalled'` event is intentionally not treated as an error because it fires transiently during normal buffering on slow connections. All `<video>` elements are paused and detached when VideoFactory.destroy is called.

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: AssetFactory implementation that loads video files (MP4, WebM, OGG, and other browser-supported container formats) and produces a Video instance suitable for use as a dynamic texture source in the rendering pipeline.

Video data is buffered via a `<video>` element rather than decoded up-front. The `'stalled'` event is intentionally not treated as an error because it fires transiently during normal buffering on slow connections. All `<video>` elements are paused and detached when VideoFactory.destroy is called.

members.name: create
members.description: Wraps video bytes in a <video> element and resolves with a Video instance once the configured loadEvent fires. Rejects if the element emits error, abort, or emptied before the load event is received.

members.name: createObjectUrl
members.description: Creates an object URL from blob and registers it for automatic cleanup when destroy is called. Prefer this over calling URL.createObjectURL directly inside a factory subclass so the URL is always tracked.

members.name: destroy
members.description: Pauses and resets all <video> elements created by this factory to release media resources, then delegates to the base AbstractAssetFactory.destroy to revoke any object URLs.

members.name: process
members.description: Reads the full response body as an ArrayBuffer for blob construction.

members.name: storageName
members.description: Identifier used as the object-store / storage-namespace key when this factory's assets are persisted to a CacheStore.
